title: ClickHouse Segment 07
summary: A large segment of ClickHouse development history covering 786 PRs across 34 dependency layers. This segment spans wide-ranging improvements including new SQL functions, MergeTree storage engine enhancements, query interpreter and analyzer advances, disk/object-storage backends, backup subsystem work, third-party library updates (contrib), build and packaging infrastructure, aggregate function optimizations, and system table additions. The changes touch virtually every major subsystem of the ClickHouse OLAP database engine.
instructionPreview: `/workspace/requirements/` contains every requirement as a separate file. Each file describes one change request. Read them all, implement every one. Order does not matter, but some changes depend on others.
instruction:
You are an autonomous engineer. The repository at /workspace is at an earlier state of a real-world project.

`/workspace/requirements/` contains every requirement as a separate file. Each file describes one change request. Read them all, implement every one. Order does not matter, but some changes depend on others.

moduleDag.description: A large segment of ClickHouse development history covering 786 PRs across 34 dependency layers. This segment spans wide-ranging improvements including new SQL functions, MergeTree storage engine enhancements, query interpreter and analyzer advances, disk/object-storage backends, backup subsystem work, third-party library updates (contrib), build and packaging infrastructure, aggregate function optimizations, and system table additions. The changes touch virtually every major subsystem of the ClickHouse OLAP database engine.

nodes.label: Common Utilities
nodes.description: Shared utility code used across all ClickHouse subsystems, including memory allocation, thread pools, hash tables, string utilities, and configuration helpers. Touched by PRs spanning all layers of the DAG.

nodes.label: Core Primitives
nodes.description: Core data structures and type definitions fundamental to the query engine, including Block, ColumnNumbers, Field types, and query settings definitions.

nodes.label: Base Libraries
nodes.description: Low-level base libraries providing platform abstraction, POSIX wrappers, and fundamental data structures used by the rest of the codebase.

nodes.label: Third-Party Dependencies
nodes.description: Vendored third-party libraries and their CMake integration, including OpenSSL, AMQP-CPP, and various compression/format libraries. Large LOC due to generated headers and library upgrades.

nodes.label: Column Engine
nodes.description: Column-oriented data containers implementing the core columnar storage model, including ColumnVector, ColumnString, ColumnArray, and nullable/low-cardinality variants.

nodes.label: Data Types
nodes.description: Type system definitions and serialization logic for all ClickHouse data types, including numeric types, strings, arrays, maps, tuples, and their serialization formats.

nodes.label: I/O Subsystem
nodes.description: Input/output abstractions including read/write buffers, compression codecs, HTTP handlers, and S3/object-storage I/O primitives.

nodes.label: Compression
nodes.description: Compression codec implementations and codec factory for data compression in storage and network transfer (LZ4, ZSTD, Delta, DoubleDelta, etc.).

nodes.label: SQL Parser
nodes.description: SQL parsing layer including lexer, parser combinators, and AST node definitions. Handles new syntax additions for functions, table engines, and DDL statements.

nodes.label: SQL Functions
nodes.description: Implementation of built-in SQL functions including scalar functions, array functions, URL functions, math functions, string functions, and type conversion functions. The largest single module by file count in this segment.

nodes.label: Aggregate Functions
nodes.description: Aggregate function implementations (sum, avg, count, quantile, minMax, etc.) including state management, merge operations, and constexpr optimizations.

nodes.label: Query Interpreters
nodes.description: Query execution interpreters that handle SELECT, INSERT, ALTER, DDL, and system queries. Central orchestration layer connecting parser output to storage and processors.

nodes.label: Query Analyzer
nodes.description: New query analysis framework (experimental analyzer) providing scope resolution, CTE handling, join analysis, and optimization passes for query planning.

nodes.label: Query Planner
nodes.description: Query planning layer that converts analyzed queries into executable pipelines, working with the new analyzer to produce optimized query plans.

nodes.label: Query Processors
nodes.description: Pipeline-based query execution engine including format processors, transforms, query plan steps, and data format implementations (JSON, CSV, Parquet, etc.).

nodes.label: Storage Engines
nodes.description: Non-MergeTree storage engine implementations including Distributed, Memory, Buffer, Kafka, and various table engine interfaces.

nodes.label: MergeTree Engine
nodes.description: The primary MergeTree family of storage engines, handling data parts, merges, mutations, indices (including ANN/Annoy), replication, and data lifecycle.

nodes.label: System Tables
nodes.description: System table implementations providing introspection into server state, metrics, parts, processes, and cluster information.

nodes.label: Disk & Object Storage
nodes.description: Disk abstraction layer including local disk, S3, Azure, HDFS object storage backends, and cached/encrypted disk wrappers.

nodes.label: Backup Subsystem
nodes.description: Backup and restore functionality including incremental backups, backup coordination, and integration with various storage backends.

nodes.label: Database Engines
nodes.description: Database-level abstractions including Atomic, Lazy, Replicated, and MaterializedMySQL database engines.

nodes.label: Access Control
nodes.description: Authentication, authorization, and access control subsystem including users, roles, quotas, row policies, and grants.

nodes.label: Server Layer
nodes.description: Server infrastructure including HTTP/TCP/gRPC handlers, connection management, and protocol implementations.

nodes.label: Table Functions
nodes.description: Table function implementations (remote, file, url, s3, numbers, etc.) that produce tables dynamically in FROM clauses.

nodes.label: Data Formats
nodes.description: Format factory and format registration for input/output data formats used in INSERT/SELECT and external data access.

nodes.label: Dictionaries
nodes.description: External dictionary implementations providing key-value lookups from various sources (files, databases, HTTP) for query enrichment.

nodes.label: Coordination (Keeper)
nodes.description: ClickHouse Keeper coordination service implementation providing ZooKeeper-compatible consensus for distributed operations.

nodes.label: Program Binaries
nodes.description: Entry points and auxiliary programs including clickhouse-server, clickhouse-client, library-bridge, and other standalone tools.

nodes.label: Utilities & CI
nodes.description: Development utilities, CI scripts, test infrastructure tooling, and build helper scripts.

nodes.label: CMake Build System
nodes.description: CMake build configuration files for compiler flags, feature detection, and third-party library integration.

edges.label: interpreters use common utilities for hashing, config, and memory
MergeTree relies on common thread pools, allocators, and hash maps
function implementations use common string and numeric helpers
disk backends use common I/O utilities and configuration
I/O layer builds on common buffer and compression primitives
interpreters depend on core Block and Field definitions
processor pipeline uses core data chunk representations
interpreters consume AST produced by the parser
analyzer resolves and validates parsed AST nodes
planner converts analyzed query trees into execution plans
planner output drives processor pipeline construction
query interpreters read/write through MergeTree storage
interpreters dispatch to various storage engine backends
interpreters interact with disk layer for DDL and data access
storage layer delegates to MergeTree for primary workloads
MergeTree persists data parts through disk abstraction
disk backends use I/O read/write buffer abstractions
MergeTree I/O operations use shared buffer infrastructure
server binary invokes interpreters for query execution
programs configure and initialize disk backends
functions operate on column data containers
columns implement storage for specific data types
CMake files integrate contributed libraries into the build
MergeTree read/write processors integrate with pipeline
backup operations use disk abstraction for storage
backups coordinate with storage engines for consistent snapshots
